domain_experience: helped compliance teams at a fintech
worked around regulator responses and compliance operations
assets: ability to turn source materials into structured draft outputs
distribution: warm outbound to small regulated firms
intro-based selling through existing compliance contacts
network_access: a few warm contacts in compliance and fintech
constraints: must avoid unauthorized practice or regulated-role overreach
limited proof of demand so far
credibility_signals: familiarity with regulator expectations and document workflows
unfair_advantages: better domain framing than a generic AI consultant
ability to package compliance-adjacent workflow support conservatively

opportunities.title: AI-enabled regulator response drafting service
opportunities.thesis: Provide first-draft regulator responses, evidence checklists, and issue logs under explicit client review and sign-off.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user has direct domain familiarity with regulator-response workflows and can deliver a more credible, safer service than a generic AI automation vendor.
validation_plan: Interview warm contacts on exam-response pain
Run a fixed-scope pilot with mandatory human review
Measure time saved and acceptance of draft quality
tool_specs: draft generator tied to source documents
kill_criteria: no measurable time savings in pilot

opportunities.title: Compliance documentation gap audit plus remediation pack
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope review of policies, evidence organization, and response readiness, then deliver a remediation roadmap and template set.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can diagnose messy compliance documentation and convert it into an actionable response-readiness package using real workflow knowledge.
validation_plan: Assess missing artifacts and evidence retrieval pain
Check whether buyers convert to ongoing drafting support
kill_criteria: clients see the audit as a one-off commodity
no follow-on work or repeatable templates emerge

opportunities.title: Regulator response workspace for small firms
opportunities.thesis: Build a narrow workflow product for intake, evidence collection, draft assembly, and approval tracking around regulator requests.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can encode the true workflow pain points rather than shipping a generic chatbot.
validation_plan: Use an internal version during service pilots
Identify the most repeated drafting and evidence-routing tasks
Test a narrow portal only after service demand is proven
kill_criteria: workflow varies too much across firms
buyers resist software but accept services only

opportunities.title: Compliance response template and playbook library
opportunities.thesis: Offer recurring access to templates, checklists, issue taxonomies, and response playbooks for common regulator interactions.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can create more practical and regulator-aware templates than generic compliance content vendors.
validation_plan: Test whether buyers want templates bundled with service work
Track which artifacts get reused across engagements
Package the most reusable items into a paid library
kill_criteria: buyers do not pay separately for templates
content becomes stale faster than it can be maintained

opportunities.title: Compliance response concierge to drafting system to workflow SaaS
opportunities.thesis: Start with a productized concierge service for response drafting and evidence coordination, convert repeated work into internal tooling, then expose only a narrow workflow product later.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: This path uses the user's strongest edge immediately, matches their warm-contact distribution, and compounds into reusable workflow assets without overclaiming regulatory authority.
validation_plan: Create a one-page offer with explicit service boundaries
Run 5 discovery calls with warm contacts
Pilot a fixed-scope engagement with mandatory client sign-off
Capture repeated workflow steps for internal tooling
kill_criteria: buyers mainly want a named outsourced compliance officer
counsel or compliance leads reject AI-assisted drafting even with review
no path from bespoke service work to reusable workflows

next_actions: Position the offer as AI-assisted regulatory response operations, not outsourced compliance judgment
Draft engagement language that requires client review, approval, and ownership
Run 5 warm discovery calls focused on exam-response pain and trust boundaries
Pilot a fixed-scope concierge engagement before building software
Document repeated steps and only productize low-risk drafting and workflow coordination tasks
